occurrenceremarks: Sunny openings in Ceanothus cuneatus-Pinus sabiniana scrub on serpentine soil in the Red Hills of Tuolumne County, CA. With only 5 phyllaries, many of these plants seem to key to L. debilis, but the Red Hills are farther north and a different habitat than reported for L. debilis in Jepson eFlora. identificationremarks: "If I had happened upon these plants in that location my assumption would be Lasthenia californica californica. CalFlora does show some occurrences of L. minor in Mendocino county. The key in Jepson says that californica has leaves that are entire while minor has ""mid-cauline leaves generally pinnately lobed"". Since I don't see any pinnately lobbed leaves in these photos I went with cal cal. I am surprised though, that these plants were still blooming on June 15, all the L. cal cal I have been seeing has dried up by now, so maybe this is something different."

occurrenceremarks: Steep slope, with a mesic environment a little later in the year. NNE facing slope has nice large patch of Quercus john-tuckeri, Q. xalvordiana, Juniperus californica, and Pinus monophylla forest. Surrounding terrain was more rolling grassy hills. Small riparian habitat with surface running water halfway down slope. Flat area near where I stopped was dry, with low dry hills above Bitter Creek. Growing on slopes.
locality: Near west end of the San Emigdio Mountains: Bitter Creek National Wildlife Refuge; North facing headwall of Bitter Creek. North of Cerro Noroeste Road, east of Pelato Peak. Southwest end of Unit #2 of Refuge map.; Ballinger Canyon Quad.7.5Started near 34.91570N, 119.38533W dropped down along an old dirt road that more or less strarting to turn to a trail. to a flat area with Artemisia tridentata, Atriplex spp. that you can see from the starting point, to 34.92206N, 119.38200W.

occurrenceremarks: Gentlly rolling hills with dry drainage crossings. Juniperus californica on some of the hills and slopes, with Yucca whipplei, Eriogonum fasciculatum polifolium, Eriophyllum confertiflorum, Ericameria linearifolia, Castilleja subinclusa on some slopes of the lower elevations. The annuals were pretty close to toast at this time in the lower elevations. Upper elevations had some grasslands, with Sambucus mexicana, Balsamorhiza deltoidea, Lomatium californicum, Agoseris grandiflora on more mesic north slopes. Upper elevations on the hilltops had patches of Lupinus formosus, L. excubitus-albifrons var. eminens complex?. Growing along dry, open slope, near base of small draw.
locality: Far west end of the San Emigdio Mountains: Bitter Creek National Wildlife refuge, south end, within map units 6, and 11. South and southwest of Cerro Noroeste Road. Just north of Ventura County line.; Ballinger Canyon 7.5 QuadCollections started in section 36, near 34.90241N 119.38576W, and ended in section 34, near 34.90862N 119.43329W. The end was along a dirt road that runs east back to the Cerro Noroeste road, and is across from Pelato Peak.

occurrenceremarks: Annual, ray and disk flowers yellow.  Occasional to locally common.
habitat: Alluvial flats and moderate to steep slopes, granitic substrate with large boulder outcrops, loose, decomposed granite soils.  Vegetation recently burned (2006 or 2007), formerly dominated by scrub of Artemisia tridentata, Chrysothamnus nauseosus, Yucca brevifolia on flats, drier slopes, scrubby woodland of Pinus sabiniana, Pinus monophylla, Quercus wislizeni, Quercus chrysolepis, Fremontodendron californicum, Ribes spp., Cercocarpus betuloides, Ceanothus vestitus, Rhamnus californica on steeper slopes and more mesic exposures.  Rich and diverse display of annuals and perennial herbs on burned area and in adjacent areas of unburned vegetation.  On burn.
locality: Sierra Nevada; Southern Sierra Nevada region: Piute Mountains, lower Esperanza Canyon at eastern base of Sorrell Peak and western edge of Kelso Valley Elev. given as 4500-4800 ft
higherclassification: Plantae, Streptophyta, Embryophyta, Spermatophyta, Angiospermae, Magnoliopsida, Asteridae, Asterales, Asteraceae
